title: Legacy001 CatBoost GPU standalone expert
summary: Train CatBoost GPU as a standalone second expert beside the legacy-001 RealMLP root and compare its OOF signal.
rationale: CatBoost can capture categorical interactions and threshold structure differently from RealMLP. Testing it as a standalone expert from the simple root gives a clean read on whether a non-neural tabular model contributes independent signal.
implementation_hint: Start from legacy-001.py. Keep the parent RealMLP path intact, add a CatBoostClassifier trained on the same folds, and report RealMLP OOF AUC, CatBoost OOF AUC, prediction correlation, and optionally a simple average. Use CatBoost GPU when agent.gpu is true with task_type='GPU' and devices='0'. Do not add LightGBM, XGBoost, stacking, calibration, or extra feature blocks.
expected_effect: Improve or at least diversify OOF predictions if CatBoost captures categorical strategy interactions missed by RealMLP.
risk: CatBoost may underperform alone or add correlated noise; GPU training can be memory-sensitive.
